title: Payment Step Friction and Purchase Completion
summary: Moderated interviews with 14 shoppers who failed to complete a purchase. The payment step was the single biggest point of loss: confusing card-entry fields, unexpected fees appearing late, and no clear sense of progress. Shoppers who were guided through a streamlined payment entry recovered their original intent to buy.

quotes.text: I had my card out and ready, but the form kept rejecting my details with no explanation, so I just gave up.
quotes.speaker: Participant 4, returning shopper

quotes.text: The extra fee only showed up on the very last screen. That's the moment I closed the tab.
quotes.speaker: Participant 9, first-time buyer
